files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.EfiRuntimeServices
files.Summary: Minimal layout of EFI_RUNTIME_SERVICES up to and including GetTime. GetTime is the first function pointer after the table header (offset 24).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.EfiSystemTable
files.Summary: Minimal layout of EFI_SYSTEM_TABLE up to and including RuntimeServices. RuntimeServices pointer is at offset 88.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.EfiTableHeader
files.Summary: Common header present at the start of every EFI table.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.EfiTime
files.Summary: EFI_TIME as defined in the UEFI specification. Size = 16 bytes.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.LimineBootTimeRequest
files.Summary: Limine Boot Time request. Provides the Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC) at the time of boot. Available on all platforms where Limine is the bootloader.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.LimineDTBRequest
files.Summary: Limine Device Tree Blob (DTB) request. Provides the address of the flattened device tree passed by firmware.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.LimineEfiSystemTableRequest
files.Summary: Limine EFI System Table request. Provides a pointer to the EFI system table, allowing access to EFI Runtime Services (e.g. GetTime) which remain valid even after ExitBootServices.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Boot.Limine.LimineExecutableCmdlineRequest
files.Summary: Limine Executable Cmdline request. Returns the kernel command line set via cmdline: in limine.conf.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.AcpiMcfgNative
files.Summary: Architecture-neutral ACPI MCFG import (PCI ECAM discovery). MADT lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64/Bridge/Import/AcpiMadtNative.cs; GIC info lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64/Bridge/Import/Gic/AcpiGicNative.cs.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.ContextSwitchNative
files.Summary: Native imports for the low-level context switch primitives. Symbols are unified across architectures: Cosmos.Kernel.Native.X64 (Interrupts.s) and Cosmos.Kernel.Native.ARM64 (ContextSwitch.s) both export the same names, so no arch gating is needed here.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.EhFrameNative
files.Summary: Native imports for the linker-provided .eh_frame section bounds. get_eh_frame_start / get_eh_frame_end are exported by the C runtime (kmain.c) and resolve to the linker symbols on both architectures, so no arch gating is needed here.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.HeapNative
files.Summary: Bridge functions that let C library code allocate from the Cosmos heap.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.IrqNative
files.Summary: Native entry point invoked from the architecture-specific IRQ assembly stubs. Forwards straight into — no indirection.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.KnobsNative
files.Summary: Native import for the runtime-provided knob values table. Consumed by the AppContext plug.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.LimineNative
files.Summary: Wrapper for C code to access managed Limine data (RSDP address, HHDM offset). NOTE: This is a data accessor wrapper - C code gets managed data, then continues in C. We do NOT call C code from managed code - only provide data access.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.MemNative
files.Summary: Bridge functions for memcpy / memcmp used by C library code.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.SerialNative
files.Summary: Bridge functions for C library code (ACPI, libc stubs) to call C# methods. NOTE: These are NOT called from C bootstrap (kmain.c) - only from library code. C bootstrap uses pure C implementations for clean architecture.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Bridge.ThreadNative
files.Summary: Stable native entry point used as the initial RIP/PC for freshly scheduled threads. The architecture-specific context-switch assembly returns (via iretq on x64 / eret on ARM64) into , which then calls the scheduler's managed entry implementation. Thread-start registration lives in SchedulerManager.RegisterThreadStart.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.ICpuOps
files.Summary: Essential CPU operations for multi-architecture support

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.IInterruptController
files.Summary: Platform-specific interrupt controller. Implementations live in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64 (APIC/IDT) and Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64 (GIC/Exception Vectors). Each owns the arch-specific dispatch path: EOI timing, ack semantics, fatal-fault handling, and any extra handler tables (e.g. GICv3 LPIs) all live in the implementation.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.IMsiBinder
files.Summary: Platform-specific MSI binding backend. Implemented once per arch.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InternalCpu.InterruptScope
files.Summary: A disposable scope that disables interrupts on creation and restores the prior interrupt-enable state on dispose. Critically, this saves the full RFLAGS / DAIF on entry and restores it on exit, so nested scopes are correct: an inner scope disposing inside an outer disabled region does NOT prematurely re-enable interrupts.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InternalCpu
files.Summary: Low-level CPU operations that can be used by Core components like the heap. Native imports live in Bridge/Import/CpuNative.cs.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InterruptManager.IrqDelegate
files.Summary: Interrupt delegate signature.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.InterruptManager
files.Summary: Architecture-neutral interrupt registration and routing. Owns the dense 256-entry handler table and the dynamic-vector allocator used by MSI programmers. The actual dispatch path (ack/EOI/LPI/fatal-fault handling) is delegated to the platform implementation in Cosmos.Kernel.Core.X64 / Cosmos.Kernel.Core.ARM64.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CPU.MsiRouting
files.Summary:
Architecture-neutral PCI MSI / MSI-X binding. Both x64 (LAPIC Fixed-mode messages) and ARM64 (GICv3 ITS GITS_TRANSLATER writes) ultimately program the same shape into a device's MSI-X table — a 64-bit address plus a 32-bit data dword — but the routing model differs:

  • x64: data = IDT vector chosen by the kernel; address encodes the destination LAPIC ID. No per-device prep.
  • ARM64 ITS: data = EventID (per-device index); address = the shared GITS_TRANSLATER doorbell. The ITS must be told the (DeviceID, EventID) → LPI mapping out of band before the device fires.

This file abstracts both behind : each platform initializer registers a binder, then HAL-level PCI MSI-X code calls once per device and per MSI-X table entry. The binder owns vector / LPI allocation and any device-specific bookkeeping (ITT allocation, MAPD, MAPTI on ARM64).

PCI device identity is passed in raw (bus, slot, function) form to keep this file free of HAL/Pci dependencies — Core can't reference HAL upstream.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.CosmosFeatures
files.Summary: Centralized feature flags for Cosmos kernel components. These flags can be set via RuntimeHostConfigurationOption in csproj and are used by ILC for trimming.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO.EarlyGop
files.Summary: Minimal early-boot GOP text renderer. Writes directly to the Limine linear framebuffer with a hardcoded 8x8 bitmap font. No back buffer, no swap, no KernelConsole dependency. Safe to call from the very first kernel instruction as long as Limine has filled in the framebuffer response (i.e. as soon as static fields are accessible).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.IO.Serial
files.Summary:
Multi-architecture serial port driver.

  • x86-64: 16550 UART via port I/O (COM1 at 0x3F8)
  • ARM64: PL011 UART via MMIO (QEMU virt at 0x09000000)

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Memory.GarbageCollector.AllocContext

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Memory.GarbageCollector.GcInfo.GcStackSlotBase

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.ConditionVariable
files.Summary: A condition variable primitive for coordinating scheduler threads around state changes.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.ContextSwitch
files.Summary: Low-level context switch operations. Native imports live in Cosmos.Kernel.Core/Bridge/Import/ContextSwitchNative.cs.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.IScheduler
files.Summary: Interface for pluggable scheduling algorithms. Inspired by Ekiben's EkibenScheduler trait.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.InterruptEvent
files.Summary:
One-shot binary completion event with auto-reset semantics, designed to be signaled from an ISR and waited on from a normal thread.

blocks the caller via until is called. only walks internal state with the spinlock held and calls — no allocation, no interface dispatch — so it is safe from interrupt context.

Modeled after but reduced to the surface drivers actually need (a producer ISR and a consumer thread). Multiple consumers can wait; wakes one. Signals are counted, not latched as a single bit: two s with two parked waiters wake both, and signals arriving while no consumer waits are consumed one per subsequent .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.IrqLockScope
files.Summary: RAII scope returned by . Disables interrupts and acquires the lock on creation; on dispose, releases the lock then restores the prior interrupt state. Dispose order matters: the lock must be released before interrupts are re-enabled so an ISR firing immediately after restore never sees the lock held.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Monitor
files.Summary: A simple monitor implementation for kernel threads.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Mutex
files.Summary: A simple mutex primitive for protecting critical sections in the scheduler.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.PerCpuState

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.SchedulerManager
files.Summary: Manages scheduler lifecycle and dispatches to current scheduler.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.SpinLock
files.Summary:
Simple spinlock for SMP synchronization.

The plain / pair is only safe for locks that are never taken from interrupt context. If an ISR can acquire the same lock as mainline code (e.g. a wait/signal pair where the ISR signals), use : a single CPU holding the plain lock when the ISR fires will spin forever waiting for itself.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.Core.Scheduler.Stride.StrideCpuData

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.Devices.Clock.RTC
files.Summary:
ARM64 Real-Time Clock implementation. Reads boot time from the PL031 RTC (ARM Primecell RTC, present on QEMU virt machine). Uses the ARM64 Generic Timer counter for sub-second elapsed time tracking.

PL031 RTCDR register provides Unix time (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC). QEMU virt machine maps PL031 at physical address 0x09010000.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.ARM64.Devices.Timer.GenericTimer

Virtqueue implementation for virtio devices. A virtqueue consists of three parts:

  • Descriptor table: array of buffer descriptors
  • Available ring: buffers offered to device
  • Used ring: buffers returned by device

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.AcpiMcfg.McfgInfo

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.AcpiMcfg

The native code is called during early boot (kmain) and parses the MCFG table to extract the PCI ECAM base address. This class just retrieves the result. Native import lives in Cosmos.Kernel.Core/Bridge/Import/AcpiNative.cs.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.AhciController
files.Summary: One initialized AHCI HBA. Each controller owns its ABAR (HHDM-mapped), capability snapshot, port list, and a per-controller command region allocated from — kernel-virtual for memset, physical (via ) for the HBA's CLB / FB / CTBA registers. The driver scans PCI for every matching SATA/AHCI controller and instantiates one per match, so a system with two HBAs gets two instances and all of their ports show up in .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Nvme
files.Summary: NVMe driver entry point. Scans PCI for every / device and brings each one up independently — a machine with two M.2 SSDs gets two instances and all their namespaces show up in .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeAdminOp
files.Summary: NVMe admin opcodes (NVM Express 1.4 §5.x).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeCns
files.Summary: Identify CNS values (NVM Express 1.4 §5.15).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeController
files.Summary:
One initialized NVMe controller. Owns its admin and a single IO queue pair, and the namespaces it discovered. The admin queue is polled (it only runs once at init); the I/O queue is interrupt-driven via MSI-X so callers of / can yield instead of burning a CPU on a spinloop, and up to -1 commands can be in flight concurrently because every slot owns its own 4 KiB DMA bounce buffer.

If MSI-X is unavailable (e.g. ARM64 today, or a pathological PCI device with no MSI-X capability) the controller falls back to polled completion, but in that mode commands are serialized through an internal mutex.

Limitations:

  • One IO submission and one IO completion queue (qid=1), depth 8. At most depth-1 commands are in flight (NVMe queue-full rule: a depth-N SQ holds N-1 entries, or the wrapped tail is indistinguishable from an empty queue).
  • Single-PRP transfers — every command moves at most one 4 KiB page through the slot's bounce buffer, so PRP2 is always 0. Namespaces whose LBA format exceeds one page (or carries metadata) are skipped at discovery.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeCqe
files.Summary: NVMe Completion Queue Entry (16 bytes). Reads only — the controller writes these.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeIoOp
files.Summary: NVMe NVM-command-set IO opcodes (NVM Express 1.4 §6.x).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeNamespace
files.Summary: One NVMe namespace exposed as an . Reads and writes are issued one LBA at a time through the parent controller's per-slot bounce buffers — multiple namespaces (or concurrent callers on the same namespace) execute in parallel up to the controller's I/O queue depth minus one (the NVMe queue-full rule).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeRegisters
files.Summary:
NVMe controller register block accessor. Wraps the BAR0 virtual base (BAR0 phys + HHDM offset) and exposes the spec registers via .

Layout follows NVM Express 1.4 §3.1.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.NvmeSqe
files.Summary: NVMe Submission Queue Entry (64 bytes). Backed by raw memory; the helpers fill the spec-defined CDWs without using managed structs (so the layout stays exactly as the controller expects regardless of runtime padding).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.PortRegisters
files.Summary: AHCI Port Registers. Carries a back-reference to the that owns the port so per-port code (SATA command issue, port reset) can reach controller state without going through globals.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.PortType
files.Summary: Port type enumeration.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Storage.Sata
files.Summary:
SATA drive port driver. The bounce buffer is a single page allocated from ; the HBA DMAs to its physical address while / touch the kernel-virtual address.

Error contract: command issue throws on failure (no free slot, device timeout, task-file error), so / never silently hand back stale bounce-buffer contents. All I/O on a port is serialized by an internal lock — the port owns a single bounce buffer.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Devices.Timer.TimerDevice
files.Summary: Abstract base class for all timer devices.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Interfaces.Devices.IBlockDevice
files.Summary:
Interface for block storage devices (SATA, NVMe, virtio-blk, etc.). Block-level read/write only — partitioning and filesystems sit above this.

Error contract: , and throw on failure (device error, timeout, out-of-range request). A return without an exception means the operation completed on the device.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Interfaces.Devices.IGraphicDevice

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Interfaces.Devices.IKeyboardDevice

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Pci.MsiX
files.Summary: Programs the MSI-X capability of a PCI device (cap ID 0x11). Architecture-neutral: the message address/data are produced by the platform-registered backend, so the same table programming works whether interrupts ultimately land on an x64 LAPIC or an ARM64 GICv3 ITS.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.HAL.Pci.MsiXContext
files.Summary: Handle returned by identifying the mapped MSI-X table for a device. Carries the platform-binder's per-device state (e.g. ARM64 ITS DeviceID + ITT pointer) so subsequent calls can route through the same context.

files.Summary: Represents the trigger handler for a .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatAttr
files.Summary: FAT directory-entry attribute bits (FAT spec).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatBootSector
files.Summary: Parsed BIOS Parameter Block plus derived geometry for a FAT volume. All *Lba fields are absolute on the underlying the BPB was read from.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatDirEntry
files.Summary: Parsed FAT directory entry. is the byte position of the 8.3 record within the buffer that produced it; LFN entries that contributed to precede it.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatDirectory
files.Summary: Parser and encoder for FAT directory clusters. Operates over raw byte spans; no I/O is performed here.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatFilesystemType
files.Summary:
FAT12 / FAT16 / FAT32 driver entry point. Pluggable into the VFS via VfsManager.RegisterFilesystem. Source strings:

  • Empty: use the injected device passed to the constructor (test seam).
  • "<index>": partition index in .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatFormatOptions
files.Summary: Parameters for FatFilesystemType.TryFormat(System.ReadOnlySpan<char>, IVfsFormatOptions?). All 0 / null fields mean "let the formatter pick a sensible default for the requested and the device size."

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatTable
files.Summary: FAT12 / FAT16 / FAT32 table accessor and chain manager. All entry reads and writes go through the underlying using geometry. Cluster numbers coming from on-disk metadata are untrusted: accessors treat anything outside the volume's data clusters as end-of-chain and never let it drive I/O.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Filesystems.Fat.FatType
files.Summary: FAT family identifier; selected from cluster count per the FAT32 spec.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Image
files.Summary: Represents a raster image.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.KernelConsole
files.Summary: Cell-based graphics console for kernel output. Uses a character grid (cells) similar to Aura OS for efficient terminal rendering. Can be instantiated on any canvas (hardware or virtual).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Graphics.Mode
files.Summary: Represents a video mode, definining its width (rows), height (columns) and color depth.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Ebr
files.Summary:
EBR (Extended Boot Record) chain parser and editor. An extended partition contains a singly-linked list of EBR sectors; each EBR holds one logical partition entry plus an optional pointer to the next EBR. Convention used:

  • Logical-entry start LBA is relative to the EBR sector that holds it.
  • Next-EBR pointer is relative to the extended partition's start LBA (the standard convention).
  • The first EBR always sits at the extended partition's start LBA.
  • Each subsequent EBR is placed immediately after the prior logical's data.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Gpt.PartitionEntry
files.Summary: Single parsed partition. Sector positions are absolute on the host disk.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Gpt
files.Summary: GPT (GUID Partition Table) parser / writer. Reads / writes the primary header at LBA 1 plus the partition entry array starting at LBA 2. 512-byte sectors assumed (matches every block device this kernel currently exposes).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Mbr.PartitionEntry
files.Summary: Single MBR partition entry. Sector positions are absolute on the host disk.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Mbr
files.Summary: MBR (Master Boot Record) partition table parser / writer. Operates on any with 512-byte sectors.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.Partition
files.Summary: Block-device view of a single partition on a host disk. Read/Write are translated to the host LBA space by adding .

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.PartitionManager.PartitionLocation
files.Summary: Identifies a partition by its absolute LBA range on the host disk.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.PartitionManager
files.Summary: High-level partition lifecycle (create / delete / resize / move) on top of and . Auto-detects the table type per call. physically copies sectors before rewriting the table; the other operations are pure table edits.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Storage.StorageManager
files.Summary: Manages block storage devices.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Timer.TimerManager
files.Summary: Manages system timers.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Vfs.IVfsDirectoryHandle
files.Summary: Managed handle for a directory node supporting lookup and mutation operations.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Vfs.IVfsFileHandle
files.Summary: Managed handle for an open file with position and byte I/O.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Vfs.IVfsNodeHandle
files.Summary: Common surface for VFS nodes (files or directories).

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Vfs.VfsManager.VfsMount
files.Summary: Represents a mounted filesystem instance.

files.Title: Cosmos.Kernel.System.Vfs.VfsManager
files.Summary: Central entry point for registering filesystem drivers and resolving VFS paths. Path-level operations (current directory, create/unlink/rename/remove, virtual root) live in the VfsManager.Paths.cs partial.

files.Title: Internal.Runtime.CompilerHelpers.LibraryInitializer
files.Summary: This class is responsible for initializing the library and its dependencies. It is called by the runtime before any managed code is executed.

files.Title: Internal.Runtime.TypeManagerHandle
files.Summary: TypeManagerHandle represents an AOT module in MRT based runtimes. These handles are a pointer to a TypeManager
